background:
As part of a study into the effects of woodland management on bird distribution, 36 woods were surveyed for the presence (P) or absence (A) of long-tailed tits and classified as one of three woodland types: pre-thicket (PT), thicket (T) or high forest (HF). The following data were obtained:

\begin{tabular}{|l|l|l|l|l|l|}
\hline Wood number & Wood type & Presence or absence of longtailed tits & Wood number & Wood type & Presence or absence of longtailed tits \\
\hline 1 & PT & P & 19 & HF & A \\
\hline 2 & HF & P & 20 & T & P \\
\hline 3 & PT & A & 21 & PT & P \\
\hline 4 & PT & P & 22 & PT & P \\
\hline 5 & HF & A & 23 & HF & A \\
\hline 6 & T & P & 24 & T & P \\
\hline 7 & HF & A & 25 & PT & A \\
\hline 8 & T & P & 26 & PT & P \\
\hline 9 & HF & P & 27 & HF & P \\
\hline 10 & T & P & 28 & T & P \\
\hline 11 & PT & A & 29 & PT & A \\
\hline 12 & T & A & 30 & PT & P \\
\hline 13 & T & P & 31 & T & P \\
\hline
\end{tabular}
\begin{tabular}{|l|l|l|l|l|l|}
\hline Wood number & Wood type & Presence or absence of longtailed tits & Wood number & Wood type & Presence or absence of longtailed tits \\
\hline 14 & HF & P & 32 & HF & A \\
\hline 15 & PT & A & 33 & T & P \\
\hline 16 & T & P & 34 & HF & A \\
\hline 17 & HF & A & 35 & PT & P \\
\hline 18 & HF & P & 36 & HF & A \\
\hline
\end{tabular}

task.query: Arrange the data in a $3 \times 2$ contingency table (including row, column and grand totals).
task.answer:
\begin{tabular}{|l|l|l|l|l|}
\hline 6.1 (i) & Pre-thicket & Thicket & High forest & Row totals \\ 
\hline Present & 7 & 10 & 5 & 22 \\ 
\hline Absent & 5 & 1 & 8 & 14 \\ 
\hline & & & & Grand total: \\ 
\hline Column totals & 12 & 11 & 13 & 36 \\ 
\hline
\end{tabular}

task.query: Calculate the expected frequencies, place them in a table and check that the row, column and grand totals agree with those for the table of observed values.
task.answer:
\begin{tabular}{|l|l|l|l|l|}
\hline (ii) & Pre-thicket & Thicket & High forest & Row totals \\ 
\hline Present & 7.333 & 6.722 & 7.944 & 22 \\ 
\hline Absent & 4.667 & 4.278 & 5.056 & 14 \\ 
\hline & & & & Grand total: \\ 
\hline Column totals & 12 & 11 & 13 & \\ 
\hline
\end{tabular}

task.query: Calculate the individual $X^2$ components and place them in a table.
Calculate the total $X^2$, df and $P$ values.
What do you conclude regarding the distribution of long-tailed tits in the different types of woodland?
task.answer: There is a significant association between forest type and the presence or absence of long-tailed tits, with tits being more likely to be present in thickets (and absent from high forests).

meta_info.book: Using Statistics to Understand the Environment
